title: Flood
description: Flash floods give minutes; river floods give hours — act on first warning.

items.text: Know your FEMA flood zone — look up your address at msc.fema.gov
Know your elevation relative to nearby flood-prone waterways
NEVER drive through standing or moving flood water — TURN AROUND DON'T DROWN
Identify evacuation routes that avoid flood-prone bridges and low areas
Important documents in waterproof, portable container ready to grab immediately
Water stored in elevated location or second story (flood water contaminates open containers)
Electrical panel, water heater, and furnace elevated if in flood zone
Flood insurance policy reviewed — standard homeowner's does NOT cover flood damage
Sandbags available for doorways and utility entries if in high-risk zone
72-hour bag packed and ready — flood evacuation can be immediate
Vehicle parked on high ground if significant flooding is forecast
Monitor NOAA forecasts — know Flash Flood Watch vs. Warning distinction
Waterproof boots for post-flood cleanup — flood water is sewage-contaminated
Document home contents with photos/video for insurance claims before flood season
Out-of-area emergency contact designated and written on paper
